title: Password Spray Response
content:
Password spraying is a low-and-slow brute force variant: attackers try a few common passwords (Password1, Winter2024, CompanyName123) against many accounts to avoid lockout thresholds.

Log patterns:
- Same password failure reason across many distinct usernames from one or few IPs
- Low failure count per user (often 1-3) spread over hours
- Targeting of cloud SSO, VPN, or OWA endpoints
- Success on a single account after hundreds of micro-attempts

Detection queries (conceptual):
- Count distinct usernames with failed password per source IP per hour
- Flag admin/service accounts with any failed password from external IP
- Alert on successful login where same IP had >10 distinct user failures in 24h

Response steps:
1. Confirm spray campaign vs misconfigured application
2. Identify compromised accounts (success after spray pattern)
3. Reset passwords and invalidate refresh tokens / sessions
4. Block source IPs at perimeter controls
5. Tune lockout: per-IP throttling in addition to per-account lockout
6. Document timeline for incident report and MITRE mapping (T1110.003)
